content.problemStatement:
Rubik’s cube has six sides in colors: white, blue, green, yellow, red, and orange. A liter of paint costs X1,X2,X3,X4,X5,X6X_1, X_2, X_3, X_4, X_5, X_6X1​,X2​,X3​,X4​,X5​,X6​ dollars for each color. Each side of the cube needs half a liter of paint to be painted. How much will you pay for coloring NNN unpainted cubes?


Input Format

The first line of the input contains a single integer TTT denoting the number of test cases. The description of TTT test cases follows.
The first line of each test case contains 7 integers NNN - number of unpainted cubes and X1,X2,X3,X4,X5,X6X_1, X_2, X_3, X_4, X_5, X_6X1​,X2​,X3​,X4​,X5​,X6​ - cost in dollars for each color.


Output Format
For each test case, output a single line containing one integer - Total cost of coloring the cube
content.inputFormat: The first line contains an integer T, the number of test cases. Each of the next T lines contains seven integers: N X1 X2 X3 X4 X5 X6, where N is the number of unpainted cubes and Xi is the cost (in dollars) of one liter of paint of the i‑th color (white, blue, green, yellow, red, orange).
content.outputFormat: For each test case output a single line containing the total amount of money (in dollars) needed to paint all N cubes.

sampleTestCases.explanation: Test case 1: sum of costs = 1+1+1+1+2+4 = 10. Cubes = 2 → liters = ceil(2/2)=1. Total = 10*1 = 10.\nTest case 2: sum = 1+2+3+4+5+6 = 21. Cubes = 3 → liters = ceil(3/2)=2. Total = 21*2 = 42.

solution.approach: For one cube we need half a liter of paint in total. Therefore N cubes need N/2 liters. Since paint can only be bought in whole liters we have to round up: liters = ceil(N/2) = (N + 1) / 2 using integer arithmetic. One liter contains all six colors, so its price is the sum of the six Xi. The answer for a test case is sumXi * liters. All operations fit in 64‑bit signed integers because max sumXi = 6·10^9 and max liters = 5·10^4, giving a product ≤ 3·10^14.
solution.timeComplexity: O(T) – constant work per test case.
solution.spaceComplexity: O(1) – only a few variables are used.

code.Python3:
#!/usr/bin/env python3
import sys

def main():
    data = sys.stdin.read().strip().split()
    if not data:
        return
    it = iter(data)
    T = int(next(it))
    out = []
    for _ in range(T):
        N = int(next(it))
        costs = [int(next(it)) for _ in range(6)]
        total = sum(costs)
        liters = (N + 1) // 2  # ceil(N/2)
        out.append(str(total * liters))
    sys.stdout.write('\n'.join(out))

if __name__ == "__main__":
    main()
